references.text: McLarnon E & The Malpighiales Team at Kew (Gill Challen, Sue Zmarzty). Interactive key to the genera of Euphorbiaceae s.l., Salicaceae and Achariaceae
Welzen, PC van & H-J Esser. 2013. Peraceae. Flora Malesiana 21: 119-133
Welzen, PC van, LJ Bulalacao & Tran Van On. 1995. A taxonomic revision of the Malesian genus Trigonopleura Hook.f. (Euphorbiaceae). Blumea 40: 363-374
Welzen, PC van. 2020. Key to the families and genera of Malesian Euphorbiaceae in the wide sense. Blumea 65: 53-60

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 September 2013

species.citation: (Elmer) Merr. , PJS 11c (1916) 77 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 453; Welzen et al., Blumea 40 (1995) 369; ≡ Alsodeia dubia Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2875; Type: Elmer 13956 (PNH, holo, lost; L, NSW, iso), Mindanao, Agusan, Cabadbaran (Mt Urdaneta). = Trigonopleura philippinensis Merr. , PJS 10c (1915) 275; Type: BS 17475 Ramos (PNH, holo, lost; K, L, iso), Samar. ≠ Trigonopleura malayana auct. non Hook.f. ; Airy Shaw, Alphab. Enum. Euph. Philip. (1983) 46.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BILIRAN, LEYTE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Surigao, SAMAR.
species.notes: Primary dipterocarp forest to logged-over forests, on shady to open places like streams and forest margins. Fl. Mar, Apr (Sep); fr. Mar-Jul. 200-1250m.
